session.h(3) Library Functions Manual session.h(3)

NAME

session.h

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct ring_consensus_t
Ring consensus state (Host-Mediated Proactive Election). struct host_liveness_t
Host liveness detection state. struct migration_ctx_t
Host migration context for tracking failover state. struct discovery_session_t
Discovery session context. struct discovery_config_t
Configuration for discovery session.

Macros


#define MAX_PARTICIPANTS 16

Typedefs


typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)
Callback to check if session should exit.

Enumerations


enum migration_state_t { MIGRATION_STATE_NONE, MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE }
Migration detection and failover state. enum discovery_state_t { DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS, DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION, DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED, DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED }
Discovery session state.

Functions


discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const discovery_config_t *config)
Create a new discovery session. void discovery_session_destroy (discovery_session_t *session)
Destroy discovery session and free resources. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start (discovery_session_t *session)
Start the discovery session. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process (discovery_session_t *session, int64_t timeout_ns)
Process session events (call in main loop). void discovery_session_stop (discovery_session_t *session)
Stop the discovery session. discovery_state_t discovery_session_get_state (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get current session state. bool discovery_session_is_active (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if session is active (call in progress). const char * discovery_session_get_string (const discovery_session_t *session)
Get session string. bool discovery_session_is_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the host. session_host_t * discovery_session_get_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Get host context (if we are host). session_participant_t * discovery_session_get_participant (discovery_session_t *session)
Get participant context (if we are participant). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_init_ring (discovery_session_t *session)
Initialize ring consensus state. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start_ring_round (discovery_session_t *session)
Start a new ring consensus round (every 5 minutes or on new joiner). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive (discovery_session_t *session)
Detect host disconnect (check connection status). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect (discovery_session_t *session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)
Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host. asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Become the host (called when elected as future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_connect_to_future_host (discovery_session_t *session)
Connect to pre-elected future host (called when NOT future host). asciichat_error_t discovery_session_get_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session, uint8_t out_id[16], char out_address[64], uint16_t *out_port, uint8_t *out_connection_type)
Get future host information. bool discovery_session_is_future_host (const discovery_session_t *session)
Check if we are the future host.

Definition at line 35 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef bool(* discovery_should_exit_fn) (void *user_data)

Callback to check if session should exit. Called periodically during blocking operations (e.g., ACDS connect, join). Should return true to gracefully cancel the current operation.

Parameters

user_data Opaque pointer provided by caller

Returns

true to cancel operation, false to continue

Definition at line 113 of file src/discovery/session.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum discovery_state_t

Discovery session state.

Enumerator

DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT
Initial state.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_ACDS
Connecting to ACDS.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CREATING_SESSION
Creating new session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_JOINING_SESSION
Joining existing session.
DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER
Waiting for peer to join (initiator).
DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING
NAT negotiation in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST
Starting as host.
DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST
Connecting to host as participant.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE
Session active (call in progress).
DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING
Host migration in progress.
DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED
Session failed.
DISCOVERY_STATE_ENDED
Session ended.

enum migration_state_t


Migration detection and failover state. SIMPLIFIED DESIGN:
    No re-election during migration! Future host was pre-elected 5 minutes ago.
    When current host dies, we already know who takes over and where to find
    them.


Enumerator
  





  
MIGRATION_STATE_NONE
    

  
No migration in progress.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED
    

  
Host disconnect detected.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER
    

  
Failing over to pre-elected future host.

  
MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE
    

  
Failover complete, call resumed.

88   MIGRATION_STATE_NONE,     
89   MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED, 
90   MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER, 
91   MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE  
92 } migration_state_t;

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_become_host
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Become the host (called when elected as future host).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 2433 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2433                                                                               {
2434   if (!session) {
2435     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2436     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2437   }
2438 
2439   log_info("Starting as new host after migration (participant ID: %02x%02x...)", session->participant_id[0],
2440            session->participant_id[1]);
2441 
2442   // Get configured port (or use default)
2443   int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
2444 
2445   // Mark ourselves as host
2446   session->is_host = true;
2447 
2448   // Create host context if needed with configured port
2449   if (!session->host_ctx) {
2450     session_host_config_t hconfig = {
2451         .port = host_port,
2452         .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
2453         .max_clients = 32,
2454         .encryption_enabled = true,
2455     };
2456 
2457     session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
2458     if (!session->host_ctx) {
2459       log_error("Failed to create host context for migration");
2460       return ERROR_MEMORY;
2461     }
2462 
2463     // Start listening for new participant connections
2464     asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
2465     if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2466       log_error("Failed to start host after migration: %d", hstart);
2467       return hstart;
2468     }
2469 
2470     log_info("Host restarted after migration, listening on port %d", host_port);
2471   }
2472 
2473   // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so other participants can reconnect
2474   acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
2475   memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2476   memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2477   SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
2478   announcement.host_port = host_port;
2479   // Set connection type based on NAT quality (for now use DIRECT_PUBLIC for localhost dev environments)
2480   announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
2481 
2482   if (session->acds_transport) {
2483     packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
2484                               sizeof(announcement), 0);
2485     log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS for new host %02x%02x... at %s:%u", announcement.host_id[0],
2486              announcement.host_id[1], announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
2487   }
2488 
2489   // Mark migration complete
2490   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2491 
2492   // Transition to ACTIVE state (host is ready)
2493   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2494 
2495   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2496 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_check_host_alive
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Detect host disconnect (check connection status).


Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK if host alive, error if disconnected


Definition at line 2310 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2310                                                                                    {
2311   if (!session || session->is_host) {
2312     // We are the host, so "host" is always alive
2313     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2314   }
2315 
2316   // For WebRTC sessions, check the WebRTC transport instead of participant_ctx
2317   if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2318     // If we have a WebRTC transport and it's marked as ready, host is alive
2319     if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
2320       return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2321     }
2322     // WebRTC transport not ready yet - might still be connecting
2323     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2324   }
2325 
2326   // For TCP sessions, check participant context
2327   // If we don't have a participant context yet, we're not connected
2328   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2329     return ERROR_NETWORK;
2330   }
2331 
2332   // Check migration state - if we're already migrating, host is definitely not alive
2333   if (session->migration.state != MIGRATION_STATE_NONE) {
2334     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Already detected disconnect
2335   }
2336 
2337   uint64_t now_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2338 
2339   // Check if we have a ping in flight that timed out
2340   if (session->liveness.ping_in_flight) {
2341     uint64_t ping_age_ms = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2342     if (ping_age_ms > session->liveness.timeout_ms) {
2343       // Ping timed out
2344       session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2345       session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
2346       log_warn("Host ping timeout (attempt %u/%u, age=%llu ms)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures,
2347                session->liveness.max_failures, (unsigned long long)ping_age_ms);
2348 
2349       // Check if we've exceeded failure threshold
2350       if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2351         log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive ping failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2352         return ERROR_NETWORK;
2353       }
2354     }
2355   }
2356 
2357   // Check if it's time to send a new ping
2358   uint64_t time_since_last_ping = now_ms - session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms;
2359   if (!session->liveness.ping_in_flight && time_since_last_ping >= session->liveness.ping_interval_ms) {
2360     // Send ping to host via participant connection
2361     socket_t host_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
2362     if (host_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2363       asciichat_error_t result = packet_send(host_socket, PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
2364       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2365         session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = now_ms;
2366         session->liveness.ping_in_flight = true;
2367         log_debug("Sent ping to host (attempt %u/%u)", session->liveness.consecutive_failures + 1,
2368                   session->liveness.max_failures);
2369       } else {
2370         log_warn("Failed to send ping to host: %d", result);
2371         session->liveness.consecutive_failures++;
2372         if (session->liveness.consecutive_failures >= session->liveness.max_failures) {
2373           log_error("Host declared dead after %u consecutive send failures", session->liveness.consecutive_failures);
2374           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2375         }
2376       }
2377     }
2378   }
2379 
2380   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2381 }

Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success

2498                                                                                          {
2499   if (!session) {
2500     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2501     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2502   }
2503 
2504   log_info("Reconnecting to future host: %s:%u (connection type: %u)", session->ring.future_host_address,
2505            session->ring.future_host_port, session->ring.future_host_connection_type);
2506 
2507   // Destroy old participant context if present
2508   if (session->participant_ctx) {
2509     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
2510     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
2511   }
2512 
2513   // Create new participant context for the future host
2514   session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
2515       .address = session->ring.future_host_address,
2516       .port = session->ring.future_host_port,
2517       .enable_audio = true,
2518       .enable_video = true,
2519       .encryption_enabled = true,
2520   };
2521 
2522   session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
2523   if (!session->participant_ctx) {
2524     log_error("Failed to create participant context for future host");
2525     return ERROR_MEMORY;
2526   }
2527 
2528   // Attempt connection to future host
2529   asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
2530   if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2531     log_error("Failed to connect to future host: %d", pconn);
2532     return pconn;
2533   }
2534 
2535   log_info("Connected to future host after migration");
2536 
2537   // Update our host info with the new future host details
2538   memcpy(session->host_id, session->ring.future_host_id, 16);
2539   SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->ring.future_host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
2540   session->host_port = session->ring.future_host_port;
2541 
2542   log_info("Updated host info to: %s:%u (id: %02x%02x...)", session->host_address, session->host_port,
2543            session->host_id[0], session->host_id[1]);
2544 
2545   // Mark migration complete (in real implementation, this would be done after successful connection)
2546   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2547 
2548   // Transition to ACTIVE state (participant is ready)
2549   set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2550 
2551   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2552 }

discovery_session_t * discovery_session_create (const
  discovery_config_t * config)


Create a new discovery session.


Parameters


Config Module Session
  configuration


Returns


New session or NULL on error


Definition at line 52 of file

52                                                                                 {
53   if (!config) {
54     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config is NULL");
55     return NULL;
56   }
57 
58   discovery_session_t *session = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(discovery_session_t), discovery_session_t *);
59   if (!session) {
60     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate discovery session");
61     return NULL;
62   }
63 
64   session->state = DISCOVERY_STATE_INIT;
65   session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
66   session->acds_transport = NULL;
67   session->peer_manager = NULL;
68   session->webrtc_transport_ready = false;
69   session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
70   session->webrtc_retry_attempt = 0;
71   session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = 0;
72   session->stun_servers = NULL;
73   session->stun_count = 0;
74   session->turn_servers = NULL;
75   session->turn_count = 0;
76 
77   // Initialize host liveness detection
78   session->liveness.last_ping_sent_ms = 0;
79   session->liveness.last_pong_received_ms = 0;
80   session->liveness.consecutive_failures = 0;
81   session->liveness.max_failures = 3;
82   session->liveness.ping_interval_ms = 3 * MS_PER_SEC_INT; // Ping every 3 seconds
83   session->liveness.timeout_ms = 10 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;      // 10 second timeout
84   session->liveness.ping_in_flight = false;
85 
86   log_info("discovery_session_create: After CALLOC - participant_ctx=%p, host_ctx=%p", session->participant_ctx,
87            session->host_ctx);
88 
89   // Load or generate identity key
90   char identity_path[256];
91   asciichat_error_t id_result = acds_identity_default_path(identity_path, sizeof(identity_path));
92   if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
93     id_result = acds_identity_load(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
94     if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
95       // File doesn't exist or is corrupted - generate new key
96       log_info("Identity key not found, generating new key");
97       id_result = acds_identity_generate(session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
98       if (id_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
99         // Save for future use
100         acds_identity_save(identity_path, session->identity_pubkey, session->identity_seckey);
101       }
102     }
103   }
104 
105   if (id_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
106     log_warn("Failed to load/generate identity key, using zero key");
107     memset(session->identity_pubkey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_pubkey));
108     memset(session->identity_seckey, 0, sizeof(session->identity_seckey));
109   } else {
110     char fingerprint[65];
111     acds_identity_fingerprint(session->identity_pubkey, fingerprint);
112     log_info("Using identity key with fingerprint: %.16s...", fingerprint);
113   }
114 
115   // Load multiple identity keys from options (multi-key protocol support)
116   const options_t *opts = options_get();
117   if (opts && opts->num_identity_keys > 0) {
118     // Load all identity keys from options
119     for (size_t i = 0; i < opts->num_identity_keys && i < MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS; i++) {
120       uint8_t pubkey[32] = {0};
121       uint8_t seckey[64] = {0};
122       asciichat_error_t key_result = acds_identity_load(opts->identity_keys[i], pubkey, seckey);
123       if (key_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
124         memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[i], pubkey, 32);
125         char fingerprint[65];
126         acds_identity_fingerprint(pubkey, fingerprint);
127         log_info("Loaded identity key #%zu with fingerprint: %.16s...", i + 1, fingerprint);
128       } else {
129         log_warn("Failed to load identity key #%zu from %s", i + 1, opts->identity_keys[i]);
130       }
131     }
132     session->num_identity_keys = opts->num_identity_keys;
133     log_info("Loaded %zu identity key(s) for multi-key protocol", session->num_identity_keys);
134   } else {
135     // Single-key mode: use only the primary identity key
136     memcpy(session->identity_pubkeys[0], session->identity_pubkey, 32);
137     session->num_identity_keys = 1;
138   }
139 
140   // ACDS connection info
141   if (config->acds_url && config->acds_url[0]) {
142     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_url, config->acds_url, sizeof(session->acds_url));
143   }
144   if (config->acds_address && config->acds_address[0]) {
145     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, config->acds_address, sizeof(session->acds_address));
146   } else {
147     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->acds_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->acds_address));
148   }
149   session->acds_port = config->acds_port > 0 ? config->acds_port : ACIP_DISCOVERY_DEFAULT_PORT;
150 
151   // Session string (if joining)
152   if (config->session_string && config->session_string[0]) {
153     SAFE_STRNCPY(session->session_string, config->session_string, sizeof(session->session_string));
154     session->is_initiator = false;
155   } else {
156     session->is_initiator = true;
157   }
158 
159   // Callbacks
160   session->on_state_change = config->on_state_change;
161   session->on_session_ready = config->on_session_ready;
162   session->on_error = config->on_error;
163   session->callback_user_data = config->callback_user_data;
164   session->should_exit_callback = config->should_exit_callback;
165   session->exit_callback_data = config->exit_callback_data;
166 
167   log_debug("Discovery session created (initiator=%d, acds=%s:%u)", session->is_initiator, session->acds_address,
168             session->acds_port);
169 
170   return session;
171 }

175     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");

177   }
178 
179   // Close ACDS connection
180   if (session->acds_transport) {
181     acip_transport_destroy(session->acds_transport);
182     session->acds_transport = NULL;
183   }
184   if (session->acds_socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
185     socket_close(session->acds_socket);
186     session->acds_socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
187   }
188 
189   // Destroy host context if active
190   if (session->host_ctx) {
191     session_host_destroy(session->host_ctx);
192     session->host_ctx = NULL;
193   }
194 
195   // Destroy participant context if active
196   if (session->participant_ctx) {
197     session_participant_destroy(session->participant_ctx);
198     session->participant_ctx = NULL;
199   }
200 
201   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (NEW)
202   if (session->peer_manager) {
203     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
204     session->peer_manager = NULL;
205   }
206 
207   // Clean up STUN/TURN server arrays (NEW)
208   if (session->stun_servers) {
209     SAFE_FREE(session->stun_servers);
210     session->stun_servers = NULL;
211     session->stun_count = 0;
212   }
213 
214   if (session->turn_servers) {
215     SAFE_FREE(session->turn_servers);
216     session->turn_servers = NULL;
217     session->turn_count = 0;
218   }
219 
220   SAFE_FREE(session);
221 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect
  (discovery_session_t * session, uint32_t disconnect_reason)


Handle host disconnect with automatic failover to future host.


Parameters


Session Session context


disconnect_reason Reason code for disconnect


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on successful failover


Definition at line 2383 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


2383                                                                                                                      {
2384   if (!session) {
2385     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
2386     return ERROR_INVALID_PARAM;
2387   }
2388 
2389   log_warn("Host disconnect detected: reason=%u", disconnect_reason);
2390 
2391   // Initialize migration context
2392   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_DETECTED;
2393   session->migration.detection_time_ms = session_get_current_time_ms();
2394   memcpy(session->migration.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2395   session->migration.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2396 
2397   // Notify ACDS of host loss (lightweight notification, no NAT data exchanged!)
2398   acip_host_lost_t host_lost = {0};
2399   memcpy(host_lost.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
2400   memcpy(host_lost.participant_id, session->participant_id, 16);
2401   memcpy(host_lost.last_host_id, session->host_id, 16);
2402   host_lost.disconnect_reason = disconnect_reason;
2403   host_lost.disconnect_time_ms = session->migration.detection_time_ms;
2404 
2405   packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_LOST, &host_lost, sizeof(host_lost), 0);
2406 
2407   // Check if we have a pre-elected future host (from last 5-minute ring round)
2408   if (session->ring.future_host_id[0] == 0) {
2409     // No future host known! This shouldn't happen if we just received FUTURE_HOST_ELECTED
2410     // within the last 5 minutes. Fall back to treating this as fatal.
2411     log_error("No future host pre-elected! Session cannot recover from host disconnect.");
2412     session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE;
2413     return ERROR_NETWORK; // Session should end
2414   }
2415 
2416   // **SIMPLIFIED**: No metrics exchange needed! Future host was already elected 5 minutes ago.
2417   // Just failover to the pre-elected host.
2418   session->migration.state = MIGRATION_STATE_FAILOVER;
2419 
2420   // Check if I am the future host
2421   if (session->ring.am_future_host) {
2422     // I become the new host immediately! (no election delay!)
2423     log_info("Becoming host (I'm the pre-elected future host)");
2424     return discovery_session_become_host(session);
2425   } else {
2426     // Connect to pre-elected future host (address already stored!)
2427     log_info("Connecting to pre-elected future host: %s:%u", session->ring.future_host_address,
2428              session->ring.future_host_port);
2429     return discovery_session_connect_to_future_host(session);
2430   }
2431 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_process
  (discovery_session_t * session, int64_t timeout_ns)


Process session events (call in main loop). Handles incoming ACDS
    messages, negotiation, and state transitions.




Parameters


Session Session context


timeout_ns Max time to wait for events in nanoseconds (0 =
  non-blocking)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error on failure


Definition at line 1553 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1553                                                                                               {
1554   if (!session) {
1555     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1556   }
1557 
1558   // Handle state-specific processing
1559   switch (session->state) {
1560   case DISCOVERY_STATE_WAITING_PEER: {
1561     // Wait for PARTICIPANT_JOINED notification from ACDS
1562     // Note: don't use a hard timeout here - let the outer process timeout (snapshot delay) control max wait
1563     // The discovery_run() loop will keep calling this until session becomes active or process exits
1564     uint64_t now_ns = time_get_ns();
1565     uint64_t wait_duration = now_ns - session->waiting_peer_start_ns;
1566 
1567     log_debug("★ WAITING_PEER: duration=%.3fs (no timeout, waiting for peer...)", wait_duration / 1e9);
1568 
1569     if (session->acds_transport && timeout_ns > 0) {
1570       // Do not set socket timeout - let receive_packet_secure use its own timeout
1571       // Setting a short socket timeout conflicts with its hardcoded 1-second timeout
1572       packet_type_t type;
1573       void *data = NULL;
1574       size_t len = 0;
1575       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1576 
1577       // Try to receive a packet
1578       asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1579           packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1580       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1581         if (type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_PARTICIPANT_JOINED) {
1582           log_info("Peer joined session, transitioning to negotiation");
1583           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING);
1584           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1585         } else {
1586           // Got some other packet, handle it or ignore
1587           log_debug("Received packet type %d while waiting for peer", type);
1588           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1589         }
1590       } else if (recv_result == ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT) {
1591         // Timeout is normal, just return
1592       } else {
1593         log_debug("Packet receive error while waiting for peer: %d", recv_result);
1594       }
1595     } else {
1596       // Fallback to sleep if no timeout or invalid transport
1597       platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
1598     }
1599     break;
1600   }
1601 
1602   case DISCOVERY_STATE_NEGOTIATING: {
1603     // Handle NAT negotiation
1604     if (session->negotiate.state == NEGOTIATE_STATE_INIT) {
1605       // Initialize negotiation
1606       negotiate_init(&session->negotiate, session->session_id, session->participant_id, session->is_initiator);
1607       log_info("Starting NAT negotiation (initiator=%d)", session->is_initiator);
1608 
1609       // Send our NETWORK_QUALITY to ACDS so peers can evaluate us
1610       asciichat_error_t send_result = send_network_quality_to_acds(session);
1611       if (send_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1612         log_warn("Failed to send initial NETWORK_QUALITY: %d", send_result);
1613       }
1614 
1615       // ACDS relays NETWORK_QUALITY only to OTHER participants, not back to sender
1616       // For now in discovery mode, assume we're the only one and become host immediately
1617       // A real implementation would wait for peer to join via PARTICIPANT_JOINED, then connect directly
1618       log_info("Proceeding as host (ACDS cannot relay peer quality in solo mode)");
1619       session->negotiate.our_quality.detection_complete = true;
1620       session->negotiate.peer_quality_received = true;
1621       memset(&session->negotiate.peer_quality, 0, sizeof(session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1622       // Mark peer quality as detected but with worst NAT type so we win host election
1623       session->negotiate.peer_quality.detection_complete = true;
1624       session->negotiate.peer_quality.nat_type = ACIP_NAT_TYPE_SYMMETRIC;
1625     }
1626 
1627     // Check for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS (in case we're waiting for host discovery)
1628     if (session->acds_transport && !session->is_initiator && !session->host_address[0]) {
1629       packet_type_t type;
1630       void *data = NULL;
1631       size_t len = 0;
1632       void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1633 
1634       asciichat_error_t recv_result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1635       if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && type == PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT) {
1636         acip_host_announcement_t *announcement = (acip_host_announcement_t *)data;
1637         log_info("Received HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS: %s:%u", announcement->host_address, announcement->host_port);
1638         SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, announcement->host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1639         session->host_port = announcement->host_port;
1640         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1641         // Continue to host role determination below
1642       } else if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1643         buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1644       }
1645     }
1646 
1647     // Determine host role (now that peer_quality is set)
1648     if (session->negotiate.peer_quality_received && session->negotiate.state != NEGOTIATE_STATE_COMPARING) {
1649       log_info("Determining host role based on NAT qualities...");
1650       asciichat_error_t result = negotiate_determine_result(&session->negotiate);
1651       if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1652         session->is_host = session->negotiate.we_are_host;
1653 
1654         if (session->is_host) {
1655           // We become the host
1656           log_info("🏠 NAT negotiation complete: WE ARE HOST (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1657                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1658                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1659           memcpy(session->host_id, session->participant_id, 16);
1660           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1661           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1662 
1663           // Fallback to localhost if NAT detection failed to determine address
1664           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1665             log_warn("NAT address empty, using localhost fallback");
1666             SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(session->host_address));
1667           }
1668 
1669           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST);
1670         } else {
1671           // They are host (shouldn't happen in solo mode, but handle it)
1672           log_info("👤 NAT negotiation complete: THEY ARE HOST at %s:%u (tier:%d vs tier:%d)",
1673                    session->negotiate.host_address, session->negotiate.host_port,
1674                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.our_quality),
1675                    nat_compute_tier(&session->negotiate.peer_quality));
1676           SAFE_STRNCPY(session->host_address, session->negotiate.host_address, sizeof(session->host_address));
1677           session->host_port = session->negotiate.host_port;
1678 
1679           // If host address is empty, wait for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS instead of using fallback
1680           if (!session->host_address[0]) {
1681             log_info("Waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT from ACDS to discover host address...");
1682             // Stay in NEGOTIATING state to continue waiting for HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT
1683             break;
1684           }
1685 
1686           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST);
1687         }
1688       } else {
1689         log_error("Failed to determine negotiation result: %d", result);
1690         set_error(session, result, "Host election failed");
1691       }
1692     }
1693     break;
1694   }
1695 
1696   case DISCOVERY_STATE_STARTING_HOST: {
1697     // Start hosting
1698     if (!session->host_ctx) {
1699       // Create host context with configured port
1700       int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1701 
1702       session_host_config_t hconfig = {
1703           .port = host_port,
1704           .ipv4_address = "0.0.0.0",
1705           .max_clients = 32,
1706           .encryption_enabled = true,
1707       };
1708 
1709       session->host_ctx = session_host_create(&hconfig);
1710       if (!session->host_ctx) {
1711         log_error("Failed to create host context");
1712         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create host context");
1713         break;
1714       }
1715 
1716       // Start listening
1717       asciichat_error_t hstart = session_host_start(session->host_ctx);
1718       if (hstart != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1719         log_error("Failed to start host: %d", hstart);
1720         set_error(session, hstart, "Failed to start host");
1721         break;
1722       }
1723 
1724       log_info("Host started, listening for connections");
1725     }
1726 
1727     // Notify listeners that we're ready
1728     if (session->on_session_ready) {
1729       session->on_session_ready(session->session_string, session->callback_user_data);
1730     }
1731 
1732     // Send HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS so participants can discover us
1733     int host_port = GET_OPTION(port);
1734     acip_host_announcement_t announcement = {0};
1735     memcpy(announcement.session_id, session->session_id, 16);
1736     memcpy(announcement.host_id, session->host_id, 16);
1737     SAFE_STRNCPY(announcement.host_address, session->host_address, sizeof(announcement.host_address));
1738     announcement.host_port = host_port;
1739     announcement.connection_type = ACIP_CONNECTION_TYPE_DIRECT_PUBLIC;
1740 
1741     if (session->acds_transport) {
1742       packet_send_via_transport(session->acds_transport, PACKET_TYPE_ACIP_HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT, &announcement,
1743                                 sizeof(announcement), 0);
1744       log_info("Sent HOST_ANNOUNCEMENT to ACDS at %s:%u", announcement.host_address, announcement.host_port);
1745     }
1746 
1747     set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1748     break;
1749   }
1750 
1751   case DISCOVERY_STATE_CONNECTING_HOST: {
1752     // Connect to host as participant
1753 
1754     // For WebRTC sessions, wait for DataChannel to open
1755     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
1756       // Check if transport is ready (DataChannel opened)
1757       if (session->webrtc_transport_ready) {
1758         log_info("WebRTC DataChannel established, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
1759         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
1760         break;
1761       }
1762 
1763       // If not ready and we haven't initiated connection yet, initiate it
1764       if (session->peer_manager && !session->webrtc_connection_initiated) {
1765         log_info("Initiating WebRTC connection to host (attempt %d/%d)...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1766                  GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts) + 1);
1767 
1768         // Initiate connection (generates offer, triggers SDP exchange)
1769         asciichat_error_t conn_result =
1770             webrtc_peer_manager_connect(session->peer_manager, session->session_id, session->host_id);
1771 
1772         if (conn_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1773           log_error("Failed to initiate WebRTC connection: %d", conn_result);
1774           set_error(session, conn_result, "Failed to initiate WebRTC connection");
1775           break;
1776         }
1777 
1778         log_info("WebRTC connection initiated, waiting for DataChannel...");
1779         session->webrtc_connection_initiated = true;
1780         session->webrtc_last_attempt_time_ms = platform_get_monotonic_time_us() / 1000;
1781       }
1782 
1783       // Check for ICE gathering timeout on all peers
1784       if (session->webrtc_connection_initiated && session->peer_manager) {
1785         int timeout_ms = GET_OPTION(webrtc_ice_timeout_ms);
1786         int timed_out_count = webrtc_peer_manager_check_gathering_timeouts(session->peer_manager, timeout_ms);
1787 
1788         if (timed_out_count > 0) {
1789           log_error("ICE gathering timeout: %d peer(s) failed to gather candidates within %dms", timed_out_count,
1790                     timeout_ms);
1791 
1792           // Check if we have retries remaining
1793           int max_attempts = GET_OPTION(webrtc_reconnect_attempts);
1794           if (session->webrtc_retry_attempt < max_attempts) {
1795             // Calculate exponential backoff delay
1796             uint32_t backoff_ms = calculate_backoff_delay_ms(session->webrtc_retry_attempt);
1797 
1798             log_warn("WebRTC connection attempt %d/%d failed, retrying in %ums...", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1799                      max_attempts + 1, backoff_ms);
1800 
1801             // Wait for backoff period
1802             platform_sleep_ms(backoff_ms);
1803 
1804             // Destroy old peer manager
1805             webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(session->peer_manager);
1806             session->peer_manager = NULL;
1807 
1808             // Re-initialize peer manager for retry
1809             asciichat_error_t reinit_result = initialize_webrtc_peer_manager(session);
1810             if (reinit_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1811               log_error("Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry: %d", reinit_result);
1812               set_error(session, reinit_result, "Failed to re-initialize WebRTC for retry");
1813 
1814               // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error
1815               if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1816                 log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1817                 set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1818                 return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1819               }
1820 
1821               break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1822             }
1823 
1824             // Reset connection state for retry
1825             session->webrtc_connection_initiated = false;
1826             session->webrtc_retry_attempt++;
1827 
1828             log_info("WebRTC peer manager re-initialized for attempt %d/%d", session->webrtc_retry_attempt + 1,
1829                      max_attempts + 1);
1830           } else {
1831             // No more retries - connection failed
1832             log_error("WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (timeout: %dms)", max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1833 
1834             // Set error state - WebRTC connection failed
1835             char error_msg[256];
1836             snprintf(error_msg, sizeof(error_msg), "WebRTC connection failed after %d attempts (%dms timeout)",
1837                      max_attempts + 1, timeout_ms);
1838             set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT, error_msg);
1839 
1840             // If --prefer-webrtc is set, this is a fatal error (no TCP fallback)
1841             if (GET_OPTION(prefer_webrtc)) {
1842               log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
1843               set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
1844               return ERROR_NETWORK_TIMEOUT;
1845             }
1846 
1847             break; // Exit this case to allow fallback to TCP
1848           }
1849         }
1850       }
1851 
1852       // Poll ACDS for WebRTC signaling messages (SDP answer, ICE candidates)
1853       // while waiting for DataChannel to open
1854       if (session->acds_transport) {
1855         // Check if data available (non-blocking with timeout_ns)
1856         struct timeval tv_timeout;
1857         tv_timeout.tv_sec = timeout_ns / NS_PER_SEC_INT;
1858         tv_timeout.tv_usec = (timeout_ns % NS_PER_SEC_INT) / 1000;
1859 
1860         fd_set readfds;
1861         FD_ZERO(&readfds);
1862         FD_SET(session->acds_socket, &readfds);
1863 
1864         int select_result = select(session->acds_socket + 1, &readfds, NULL, NULL, &tv_timeout);
1865         if (select_result > 0 && FD_ISSET(session->acds_socket, &readfds)) {
1866           // Data available to read
1867           packet_type_t type;
1868           void *data = NULL;
1869           size_t len = 0;
1870           void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1871 
1872           asciichat_error_t recv_result =
1873               packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
1874 
1875           if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1876             // Dispatch to appropriate handler
1877             handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
1878             buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1879           } else {
1880             log_debug("Failed to receive ACDS packet: %d", recv_result);
1881           }
1882         }
1883       }
1884 
1885       // Stay in CONNECTING_HOST until DataChannel opens (webrtc_transport_ready becomes true)
1886       // The discovery_on_transport_ready callback will set webrtc_transport_ready=true
1887       break;
1888     }
1889 
1890     if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1891       // Create participant context for this session
1892       session_participant_config_t pconfig = {
1893           .address = session->host_address,
1894           .port = session->host_port,
1895           .enable_audio = true,
1896           .enable_video = true,
1897           .encryption_enabled = true,
1898       };
1899 
1900       session->participant_ctx = session_participant_create(&pconfig);
1901       if (!session->participant_ctx) {
1902         log_error("Failed to create participant context");
1903         set_error(session, ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create participant context");
1904         break;
1905       }
1906 
1907       // Attempt connection
1908       asciichat_error_t pconn = session_participant_connect(session->participant_ctx);
1909       if (pconn != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1910         log_error("Failed to connect as participant: %d", pconn);
1911         // Stay in this state, will retry on next process() call
1912         break;
1913       }
1914 
1915       log_info("Connected to host, performing crypto handshake...");
1916 
1917       // Perform crypto handshake with server
1918       // Get the socket from participant context
1919       socket_t participant_socket = session_participant_get_socket(session->participant_ctx);
1920       if (participant_socket == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1921         log_error("Failed to get socket from participant context");
1922         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to get participant socket");
1923         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1924         break;
1925       }
1926 
1927       // Step 1: Send client protocol version
1928       log_debug("Sending protocol version to server...");
1929       protocol_version_packet_t client_version = {0};
1930       client_version.protocol_version = HOST_TO_NET_U16(1);  // Protocol version 1
1931       client_version.protocol_revision = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0); // Revision 0
1932       client_version.supports_encryption = 1;                // We support encryption
1933       client_version.compression_algorithms = 0;
1934       client_version.compression_threshold = 0;
1935       client_version.feature_flags = 0;
1936 
1937       int result = send_protocol_version_packet(participant_socket, &client_version);
1938       if (result != 0) {
1939         log_error("Failed to send protocol version to server");
1940         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send protocol version");
1941         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1942         break;
1943       }
1944 
1945       // Step 2: Receive server protocol version
1946       log_debug("Receiving server protocol version...");
1947       packet_type_t packet_type;
1948       void *payload = NULL;
1949       size_t payload_len = 0;
1950 
1951       int recv_result = receive_packet(participant_socket, &packet_type, &payload, &payload_len);
1952       if (recv_result != ASCIICHAT_OK || packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_PROTOCOL_VERSION) {
1953         log_error("Failed to receive server protocol version (got type 0x%x)", packet_type);
1954         if (payload) {
1955           buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1956         }
1957         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive protocol version from server");
1958         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1959         break;
1960       }
1961 
1962       if (payload_len != sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t)) {
1963         log_error("Invalid protocol version packet size: %zu", payload_len);
1964         buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1965         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Invalid protocol version packet");
1966         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1967         break;
1968       }
1969 
1970       protocol_version_packet_t server_version;
1971       memcpy(&server_version, payload, sizeof(protocol_version_packet_t));
1972       buffer_pool_free(NULL, payload, payload_len);
1973 
1974       if (!server_version.supports_encryption) {
1975         log_error("Server does not support encryption");
1976         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Server does not support encryption");
1977         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
1978         break;
1979       }
1980 
1981       log_info("Server protocol version: %u.%u (encryption: yes)", NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_version),
1982                NET_TO_HOST_U16(server_version.protocol_revision));
1983 
1984       // Step 3: Send crypto capabilities
1985       log_debug("Sending crypto capabilities...");
1986       crypto_capabilities_packet_t client_caps = {0};
1987       client_caps.supported_kex_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001);    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1988       client_caps.supported_auth_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0003);   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519 | AUTH_ALGO_NONE
1989       client_caps.supported_cipher_algorithms = HOST_TO_NET_U16(0x0001); // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1990       client_caps.requires_verification = 0;
1991       client_caps.preferred_kex = 0x0001;    // KEX_ALGO_X25519
1992       client_caps.preferred_auth = 0x0001;   // AUTH_ALGO_ED25519
1993       client_caps.preferred_cipher = 0x0001; // CIPHER_ALGO_XSALSA20_POLY1305
1994 
1995       result = send_crypto_capabilities_packet(participant_socket, &client_caps);
1996       if (result != 0) {
1997         log_error("Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1998         set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send crypto capabilities");
1999         session_participant_disconnect(session->participant_ctx);
2000         break;
2001       }
2002 
2003       log_info("Crypto handshake initiated successfully");
2004       log_warn("*** Connected to host as participant - transitioning to ACTIVE ***");
2005     }
2006 
2007     // Check if connection is established
2008     bool ctx_valid = session->participant_ctx != NULL;
2009     bool is_connected = ctx_valid ? session_participant_is_connected(session->participant_ctx) : false;
2010     log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - participant_ctx=%p, ctx_valid=%d, is_connected=%d",
2011               session->participant_ctx, ctx_valid, is_connected);
2012 
2013     if (ctx_valid && !is_connected) {
2014       // Log more details about why it's not connected
2015       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - context exists but not connected yet");
2016     }
2017 
2018     if (is_connected) {
2019       log_info("Participant connection confirmed, transitioning to ACTIVE state");
2020       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2021     } else {
2022       log_debug("discovery_session_process: CONNECTING_HOST - awaiting connection establishment (ctx_valid=%d, "
2023                 "is_connected=%d)",
2024                 ctx_valid, is_connected);
2025     }
2026     break;
2027   }
2028 
2029   case DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE:
2030     // Receive and handle ACDS packets for WebRTC signaling (NEW)
2031     if (session->session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC && session->acds_transport) {
2032       // Use non-blocking polling to check for incoming ACDS packets
2033       struct pollfd pfd = {.fd = session->acds_socket, .events = POLLIN, .revents = 0};
2034 
2035       int poll_result = socket_poll(&pfd, 1, 0); // Non-blocking poll (timeout=0)
2036 
2037       if (poll_result > 0 && (pfd.revents & POLLIN)) {
2038         // Data available to read
2039         packet_type_t type;
2040         void *data = NULL;
2041         size_t len = 0;
2042         void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
2043 
2044         asciichat_error_t recv_result =
2045             packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &type, &data, &len, &alloc_buffer);
2046 
2047         if (recv_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2048           // Dispatch to appropriate handler
2049           handle_acds_webrtc_packet(session, type, data, len);
2050           buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
2051         }
2052       }
2053     }
2054 
2055     // Session is active - check for host disconnect
2056     if (!session->is_host) {
2057       // We are a participant - check if host is still alive
2058       asciichat_error_t host_check = discovery_session_check_host_alive(session);
2059       if (host_check != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2060         // Host is down! Trigger automatic failover to pre-elected future host
2061         log_warn("Host disconnect detected during ACTIVE state");
2062 
2063         // If --prefer-webrtc is set, WebRTC failure is fatal (no TCP fallback)
2064         const options_t *opts = options_get();
2065         if (opts && opts->prefer_webrtc) {
2066           log_fatal("WebRTC connection failed and --prefer-webrtc is set - exiting");
2067           set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2068           session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2069           return ERROR_NETWORK;
2070         }
2071 
2072         set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING);
2073         // Reason code: 1 = timeout/disconnect detected during ACTIVE session
2074         discovery_session_handle_host_disconnect(session, 1);
2075       }
2076     }
2077     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2078     break;
2079 
2080   case DISCOVERY_STATE_MIGRATING:
2081     // Host migration in progress
2082     // Check if migration has timed out (if it takes >30 seconds, give up)
2083     if (session->migration.state == MIGRATION_STATE_COMPLETE) {
2084       // Migration completed successfully
2085       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_ACTIVE);
2086       log_info("Migration complete, session resumed");
2087     } else if (session_get_current_time_ms() - session->migration.detection_time_ms > 30 * MS_PER_SEC_INT) {
2088       // Migration timed out after 30 seconds
2089       log_error("Host migration timeout - session cannot recover");
2090       set_state(session, DISCOVERY_STATE_FAILED);
2091       session->error = ERROR_NETWORK;
2092     }
2093     platform_sleep_us(timeout_ns / 1000);
2094     break;
2095 
2096   default:
2097     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid session state");
2098     break;
2099   }
2100 
2101   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
2102 }

asciichat_error_t discovery_session_start
  (discovery_session_t * session)


Start the discovery session. Connects to ACDS and either creates
    or joins a session.




Parameters


Session Session context


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success


Definition at line 1356 of file
    src/discovery/session.c.


1356                                                                         {
1357   log_info("discovery_session_start: ENTRY - session=%p", session);
1358 
1359   if (!session) {
1360     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "session is NULL");
1361   }
1362 
1363   log_info("discovery_session_start: is_initiator=%d, session_string='%s'", session->is_initiator,
1364            session->session_string);
1365 
1366   // Check if we should exit before starting blocking operations
1367   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1368     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (before ACDS connect)");
1369     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1370   }
1371 
1372   log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling connect_to_acds...");
1373   // Connect to ACDS
1374   asciichat_error_t result = connect_to_acds(session);
1375   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1376     log_error("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds FAILED - result=%d", result);
1377     return result;
1378   }
1379   log_info("discovery_session_start: connect_to_acds succeeded");
1380 
1381   // Check again after connection
1382   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1383     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after ACDS connect)");
1384     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1385   }
1386 
1387   // Perform crypto handshake with ACDS (same as client-server handshake)
1388   log_info("discovery_session_start: Performing crypto handshake with ACDS...");
1389   crypto_handshake_context_t handshake_ctx;
1390   memset(&handshake_ctx, 0, sizeof(handshake_ctx));
1391 
1392   result = crypto_handshake_init("discovery_client", &handshake_ctx, false);
1393   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1394     log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake init failed - %s", asciichat_error_string(result));
1395     set_error(session, result, "Crypto handshake init failed");
1396     return result;
1397   }
1398 
1399   // Store identity key in handshake context
1400   handshake_ctx.client_public_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1401   handshake_ctx.client_private_key.type = KEY_TYPE_ED25519;
1402   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_public_key.key, session->identity_pubkey, 32);
1403   memcpy(handshake_ctx.client_private_key.key.ed25519, session->identity_seckey, 64);
1404 
1405   // Step 0: Receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS from ACDS
1406   {
1407     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1408     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1409     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1410     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1411 
1412     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1413     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK || pkt_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_PARAMETERS) {
1414       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS (got type %u)", pkt_type);
1415       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1416       set_error(session, result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
1417                 "Failed to receive CRYPTO_PARAMETERS");
1418       return result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? result : ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1419     }
1420 
1421     if (pkt_len != sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t)) {
1422       log_error("discovery_session_start: Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size: %zu (expected %zu)", pkt_len,
1423                 sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1424       buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1425       set_error(session, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid CRYPTO_PARAMETERS size");
1426       return ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL;
1427     }
1428 
1429     crypto_parameters_packet_t server_params;
1430     memcpy(&server_params, pkt_data, sizeof(crypto_parameters_packet_t));
1431     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1432 
1433     // Pass params in network byte order — crypto_handshake_set_parameters
1434     // handles conversion internally for client (is_server=false)
1435     result = crypto_handshake_set_parameters(&handshake_ctx, &server_params);
1436     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1437       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to set crypto parameters");
1438       set_error(session, result, "Failed to set crypto parameters");
1439       return result;
1440     }
1441     log_info("discovery_session_start: CRYPTO_PARAMETERS received and applied");
1442   }
1443 
1444   // Step 1: Key exchange - receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT and respond
1445   {
1446     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1447     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1448     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1449     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1450 
1451     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1452     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1453       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT");
1454       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange recv failed");
1455       return result;
1456     }
1457 
1458     result = crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1459     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1460 
1461     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1462       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake key exchange failed");
1463       set_error(session, result, "Handshake key exchange failed");
1464       return result;
1465     }
1466   }
1467 
1468   // Step 2: Auth response - receive AUTH_CHALLENGE and respond
1469   {
1470     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1471     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1472     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1473     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1474 
1475     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1476     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1477       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive AUTH_CHALLENGE");
1478       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response recv failed");
1479       return result;
1480     }
1481 
1482     result =
1483         crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1484     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1485 
1486     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1487       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake auth response failed");
1488       set_error(session, result, "Handshake auth response failed");
1489       return result;
1490     }
1491   }
1492 
1493   // Step 3: Complete handshake
1494   // Note: Some servers (like ACDS without auth) complete the handshake in step 2,
1495   // so check if we're already done before trying to receive another packet
1496   if (handshake_ctx.state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY) {
1497     packet_type_t pkt_type;
1498     void *pkt_data = NULL;
1499     size_t pkt_len = 0;
1500     void *alloc_buffer = NULL;
1501 
1502     result = packet_receive_via_transport(session->acds_transport, &pkt_type, &pkt_data, &pkt_len, &alloc_buffer);
1503     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1504       log_error("discovery_session_start: Failed to receive HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE");
1505       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete recv failed");
1506       return result;
1507     }
1508 
1509     result = crypto_handshake_client_complete(&handshake_ctx, session->acds_transport, pkt_type, pkt_data, pkt_len);
1510     buffer_pool_free(NULL, alloc_buffer, 0);
1511 
1512     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1513       log_error("discovery_session_start: Handshake complete failed");
1514       set_error(session, result, "Handshake complete failed");
1515       return result;
1516     }
1517   } else {
1518     log_info("discovery_session_start: Handshake already complete from auth_response step");
1519   }
1520 
1521   log_info("discovery_session_start: Crypto handshake with ACDS completed");
1522 
1523   // Check again after handshake
1524   if (session->should_exit_callback && session->should_exit_callback(session->exit_callback_data)) {
1525     log_info("discovery_session_start: Exiting early due to should_exit_callback (after handshake)");
1526     return ASCIICHAT_OK; // Return success to allow clean shutdown
1527   }
1528 
1529   // Create or join session
1530   if (session->is_initiator) {
1531     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling create_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1532     result = create_session(session);
1533     log_info("discovery_session_start: create_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, result=%d",
1534              session->participant_ctx, result);
1535 
1536     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1537       // Initiator also needs to register itself as a participant so it can receive PARTICIPANT_JOINED notifications
1538       // when other peers join. This makes the initiator discoverable to other participants.
1539       log_info("Initiator: now registering self as participant in created session");
1540       result = join_session(session);
1541       log_info("Initiator: join_session result=%d", result);
1542     }
1543     return result;
1544   } else {
1545     log_info("discovery_session_start: Calling join_session - participant_ctx before=%p", session->participant_ctx);
1546     asciichat_error_t result = join_session(session);
1547     log_info("discovery_session_start: join_session returned - participant_ctx after=%p, state=%d, result=%d",
1548              session->participant_ctx, session->state, result);
1549     return result;
1550   }
1551 }

2108   }
2109 
2110   log_info("Stopping discovery session...");
2111 
2112   // Send SESSION_LEAVE if connected

2114     acip_session_leave_t leave_msg;
